title: Notion Mail is shutting down as Notion leans into email agents
friendlyTitle: Notion Mail is closing because Notion wants agents to handle email
summary: TechCrunch reports that Notion will shut down Notion Mail on September 22, 2026, saying more users are handing email workflows to agents rather than opening a traditional inbox. Gmail messages remain intact, but users may need to export drafts, scheduled emails, snippets, and auto-label instructions.
friendlySummary: Notion is closing its email app and focusing on AI agents that manage email tasks. Gmail messages stay safe, but some Notion Mail data may need exporting.
whyItMatters: Email is a useful early test for everyday agents because it combines routine actions, personal context, and high error cost. Users should treat the shutdown as a reminder to check export paths, fallback workflows, and which automations will keep running when a product changes direction.
friendlyWhyItMatters: If you rely on AI email tools, make sure you can save your drafts, rules, and settings. AI features are useful, but products can change quickly.
